title: Dizzy Gillespie - Jazz Greats
subTitle: American jazz trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie was an influential figure of bebop known for fusing Cuban, Latin American, and Carribean influences in his music.

subTitle: Watch the performance of Jamie Cullum live at Jazz à Vienne, in 2017.

subTitle: Vocalist Jazzmeia Horn's 2019 Malta Jazz Festival appearance; a celebration of jazz's future, infused with the soul of its past.

subTitle: Art Blakey's Jazz Messengers performs in Olympia Hall in Paris, France on March 16, 1963.

subTitle: For his 2018 performance at Théâtre de Villefranche-sur-Saône, Omar Sosa brings to stage his Quarteto Afrocubano members Childo Tomas, Ernesto Simpson and Leandro Saint-Hill.

title: Stingray Djazz
subTitle: In 1968, the Newport Jazz Festival in Rotterdam welcomed three talented Americans; pianist Horace Silver, guitarist Muddy Waters and trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie, alongside their bands.

title: Lalah Hathaway
subTitle: See Grammy Award winner Lalah Hathaway transform the 2018 Sing Jazz Festival scene into a real R&B club.

title: John Coltrane: Live in Comblain-la-Tour
subTitle: John Coltrane's 1965 performance at the short-lived Belgian jazz festival in Comblain-la-Tour features Trane's classic quartet with Elvin Jones, Jimmy Garrison and McCoy Tyner.

title: Bandit 65 - Jazzfest Bonn
subTitle: Bandit 65 is the breathtakingly virtuoso trio of guitarists Kurt Rosenwinkel and Tim Motzer with percussionist Gintas Janusonis. With great power and astounding beauty they explore the intersection of rhythm and soundscape in free improvisations.

title: Duke Ellington and His Orchestra
subTitle: Duke Ellington, the foremost jazz composer and bandleader, returns to the festival where he revived his career in 1956, performing in Brussels in 1973.

title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1980, American soul musician Ray Charles brought The Raelettes and the Ray Charles Orchestra to the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague, Netherlands; with them, Charles shows why he is viewed as a recognizable voice.

title: Chick Corea and Friends
subTitle: The musician performs with Bob Berg, Eddie Gomez, Steve Gadd.

title: The Story of Jazz
subTitle: Performances, commentary and compelling historic insights provide an overview of the cross-cultural influences that produced the constantly evolving sounds of jazz music.

title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1982, American jazz fusion band Spyro Gyra took the stage at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague; having struck gold with their album "Morning Dance" just a few years prior, they played a slick set with exuberant energy.

title: The Great Charlie Galbraith All Star Jazz Band
subTitle: In 1963, the small Belgian town of Comblain-la-Tour hosted the talented groups of Charlie Galbraith, Knut Kiesewetter, Bud Shank and Pim Jacobs and Marc Laferrière in a jazz festival that mixed new and old styles.

title: Stingray Djazz
subTitle: In 2019, the city of Sofia, Bulgaria, hosted the International A to JazZ Festival; among the featured acts is the jazz trumpeter Christian Scott aTunde Adjuah.

title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1986, the third version of jazz fusion band Mahavishnu Orchestra, comprised of guitarist John McLaughlin, pianist Jim Beard, bassist Jonas Hellborg, drummer Danny Gottlieb and saxophonist Bill Evans, played at the North Sea Jazz Festival.

title: Rhoda Scott & La Velle: Jazz à Vienne
subTitle: Although American soul and jazz organist Rhoda Scott spent most of her career in France, this performance at the antique theatre of Vienne is special: she presents an intimate and beautiful performance with US gospel and blues singer La Velle.

title: Hackensack
subTitle: Explore the music of John Coltrane; immerse yourself in the collection of performances from the early 1960s that showcase Coltrane's artistry.

title: Sammy Price and the Harlem Beggars
subTitle: Gospel singers known as the "Harlem Beggars" join pianist, singer and civil rights activist Sammy Price.

title: Avishai Cohen at Lviv Ukraine 2017
subTitle: Avishai Cohen's trio with Noam David and Omri Mor join forces with the International Symphony Orchestra INSO-Lviv, conducted by Christian Schumann.

title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1986, multi-talented American guitarist John Scofield and his band performed a concert at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague; first known for playing with Miles Davis, Scofield shows here that he earned that pedigree.

title: Ryan Porter & Kamasi Washington -- Ryan Porter & the West Coast Get Down -- BIRD
subTitle: Saxophonist Kamasi Washington and trombonist Ryan Porter co-found West Coast Get Down, a musical collective fusing jazz and funk with hip-hop; the two musicians join forces and bring the vibes of Los Angeles to Rotterdam, the Netherlands.

title: Donna Lee
subTitle: The audience at the Montreal Jazz Festival in Canada was in for a treat on July 3, 1982, as a true musical innovator hit the stage; a look at Jaco Pastorius, who transformed the electric bass into a formidable solo instrument.

title: Stingray Djazz
subTitle: Two Belgian musicians, guitarist Philip Catherine and bassist Jean-Louis Rassinfosse, accompany American jazz trumpeter and vocalist Chet Baker during this 1985 performance at the Brussels Jazz Club.

title: Jacques Loussier in Lepzig: Play Bach and More
subTitle: On July 28th 2004, the day of Bach's 254th anniversary of death, the Jacques Loussier Trio perform a special concert at the famous St. Thomas Church of Leipzig.

title: Fannie Mae
subTitle: Jaco Pastorius, a true musical innovator, takes the stage at the Montreal Jazz Festival; he is transforming the electric bass into a formidable solo instrument, wowing the audience in Canada with his unique style and talent.

title: Live in Palais des Beaux-Arts - Thelonious Monk, Charlie Rouse, Frankie Dunlop & John Ore
subTitle: Thelonious Monk performs live in Belgium in the winter of 1965.

title: Live in Belgium - Thelonious Monk, Charlie Rouse, John Ore & Frankie Dunlop
subTitle: Thelonious Monk: Live in '65 features an intimate concert filmed three days apart in Belgium in the Winter of 1965 with a legendary quartet that includes drummer Frankie Dunlop, bassist John Ore and tenor saxophonist Charlie Rouse.

title: Song for my Father
subTitle: Horace Silver created a jazz classic honoring his father. The piece has a catchy tune with bossa nova feel and expresses deep emotion.

title: Lady Day: The Many Faces of Billie Holiday
subTitle: Film clips show the jazz/blues artist performing "God Bless the Child" and "Please Don't Talk About Me When I'm Gone."

title: Mack the knife
subTitle: This selection of unforgettable performances by some of the most iconic and influential male jazz and pop vocalists in history showcases these legends' unique essence.

title: Live at Estival Lugano - Franco Ambrosetti, George Gruntz, Steve Coleman & J.F. Clark
subTitle: Since 1977, Estival is a summer jazz festival in Lugano, Switzerland; Estival offers a thrilling and particularly surprising line-up that explores the rich world of contemporary music whilst promoting tolerance, and co-existence.

title: Dhafer Youssef -- Jazz à Vienne 2018
subTitle: In 2018, Tunisian composer Dhafer Youssef alternated between his voice and oud playing at Jazz à Vienne; his abilities have to be heard to be believed; he is joined by bassist Matt Brewer and drummer Aaron Parks.

title: Chucho Valdes at Bata Jazz Malta 2019
subTitle: Cuban pianist Chucho Valdés brings his band Jazz Batá Two to the Malta Jazz Festival on July 19, 2019; they deliver a performance that reverberates through the historic island.

title: Newport Jazz Festival 1968
subTitle: In 1968, jazz's greatest artists appeared at the third Newport Jazz Festival in Rotterdam. This broadcast features the bands of pianist Dave Brubeck, vibraphonists Gary Burton and Red Norvo, and trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie.

title: Estival Jazz Lugano
subTitle: In 1996, renowned American saxophonist Wayne Shorter and his quintet perform at Estival Jazz Lugano; together with pianist Jim Beard, guitarist David Gilmore, bassist Alphonso Johnson and drummer Rodney van Holmes.

title: Vigil
subTitle: Explore the music of an influential and innovative saxophonist in jazz: John Coltrane; get immersed in this collection of performances from the early 1960s that showcase Coltrane's artistry.

title: Stingray Djazz
subTitle: Bootsy Collins' syncopated, hard and relentlessly rhythmic music strongly influenced funk music's development; in his 60s, Bootsy maintains that same energy, playing a show at Jazz à Vienne.

title: Marcus Miller: Thoughts on Miles
subTitle: In the 2009 portrait of Marcus Miller, Miller looks back on his relationship with Miles Davis; he shares stories of the recording process of the album "Tutu," explaining how he wrote the title track.

title: Thelonious Monk: Jazz Greats
subTitle: The idiosyncratic pianist and composer Thelonious Monk in a performance with his quartet in Copenhagen, Denmark on April 17, 1966 and Warsaw, Poland on April 4, 1966.

title: Stingray Djazz
subTitle: Features a concert from Salif Keita, a talented musician who combines Malian, European and American influences by using a range of both West African and Western European musical instruments.

title: Guitarlegend in The Netherlands
subTitle: Wes Montgomery: Live in '65 shines a light on one of the unique and influential guitarists in music history. This rare footage illuminates Wes's extraordinary musical vocabulary and unconventional picking technique.

title: Michel Legrand Orchestra: Spa, 1982
subTitle: Michel Legrand and his orchestra perform a number of his soundtrack compositions during a spirited 1982 performance in the Belgian town of Spa.

subTitle: This selection of performances by some of the most influential jazz guitarists in history showcases their unique essence.

title: Adjustment
subTitle: Jazz pianist Horace Silver's fusion of hard bop, blues, soulful grooves and Cape Verdean influences has left a mark on the world of jazz.

title: Live in Australia - Louis Armstrong, Billy Kyle, Trummy Young & Aervell Shaw
subTitle: In 1964 Louis Armstrong recorded his biggest-selling record, Hello, Dolly! He made assorted television appearances, especially in the 1950s and 1960s - the recorded film was a TV Show in Australia when Armstrong was at the peak of his career.
